title: 3D Renders Using AI: Turning Text Prompts into Stunning Visuals!
description:
Welcome to the newest way of making designs on the computer!
In this video, we'll explore how computers, using something called AI, can make 3D pictures from words we type. Have you ever thought about turning words into real-looking pictures?

I use a tool called "Illustrate Ai" to make amazing 3D pictures. If you like what you see, please press the 'Like' button. And if you want to learn more about AI, subscribe to my channel!

Prompts also work with stable diffusion sdxl model

Join our Community: https://www.facebook.com/groups/pixaromacommunity

Chapters
00:00 Introduction to 3D Renders
00:46 3D Software and Engines List
01:10 3D Render Styles
01:21 3D Render Prompts
transcript: Hello and welcome to another video tutorial. Today we'll discuss how to use Illustrate AI to  create various types of 3D renders. A 3D render is similar to capturing an image of a digital model.  Picture this, you set up a virtual scene on your computer. You add elements like a cube,  a floor, lights, materials, and various colors. Once everything looks perfect, you snap an image  of that scene using a virtual camera. This captured image is what we refer to as a 3D render.  It's a technique widely used in movies, video games, and design to visualize how something  will appear before it's actually made. On the left, you can see the model, while on the right,  you'll see the 3D render, the final image of that scene. To create 3D renders in Illustrate AI,  utilize the art style named 3D model. To introduce variation in the images you generate,  include names of 3D software and 3D render engines in your prompt. From my experiments,  these changes might be subtle. However, by using a unique seed number and simply switching out  the 3D software name in the prompt, you can achieve these subtle variations. Combining various  3D related terms can enhance the outcome, guiding the system towards your preferred visual style.  I'll share additional terms you can incorporate into your prompt. Feel free to pause the video  to delve deeper into them. Afterward, I'll display some actual prompt examples along with the results  I obtained.
